target.name: Wiliot
target.founder: Tal Tamir, Yaron Elboim, Alon Yehezkely

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $364M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B

private.name: DEEPX
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
Wiliot is a Series C semiconductor company founded by Tal Tamir, Yaron Elboim, and Alon Yehezkely, having raised $364M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 11 peers: 3 public companies (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private firms in the Chips & Semiconductors sector, including d-Matrix, Tenstorrent, Etched, Positron AI, and DEEPX.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, treating total raised as a valuation proxy, applies a typical Series C step-up multiplier of 1.7x to $364M, yielding $619M. Peer-median analysis examines five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $529M (DEEPX, Series C) to $5.0B (Etched, Series B), producing a median of $2.0B. The dispersion reflects variance in technology focus and funding stage across the peer set.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $526M to $2.3B, with a median estimate of $1.3B. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound, while peer comparables—particularly d-Matrix and Tenstorrent at Series C/D stages—anchor the upper range. Key uncertainties include capital intensity at later-stage development, customer traction visibility, and whether Wiliot's specific application space commands stage-appropriate multiples relative to broader semiconductor infrastructure peers.
